title: pancheria communis
intro: Pancheria communis is a species of flowering plant in the family Cunoniaceae, commonly known as the native currant. It is native to the coastal regions of eastern Australia, where it is found in a variety of habitats, including forests and rocky outcrops. Pancheria communis has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which have been shown to have potential health benefits, including the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory infections. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and has also been used as a topical application to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
